name: azure-ai-formrecognizer-java
description: Build document analysis applications with Azure Document Intelligence (Form Recognizer) SDK for Java. Use when extracting text, tables, key-value pairs from documents, receipts, invoices, or building custom document models.
capabilities: Extract text, tables, and selection marks from documents using prebuilt-layout model
Analyze receipts, invoices, business cards, and ID documents with specialized prebuilt models
Build custom document models trained on your specific form types
Classify documents into categories using custom document classifiers
Process documents from files or URLs with async polling operations
Retrieve key-value pairs and structured fields from general documents
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The file is Markdown documentation containing Java SDK code examples. No executable code, command injection, or malicious patterns detected. External command detections confused markdown code fences with shell syntax. URL references are documentation examples for Azure endpoint configuration.
valueStatement: Automate document processing by extracting text, tables, and structured data from forms, invoices, and receipts. Build Java applications that leverage Azure Document Intelligence prebuilt models or create custom document analyzers.
userTitle: Extract Data from Documents with Azure Form Recognizer
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Form Recognizer resource with valid endpoint and credentials
Custom model training requires labeled training documents stored in Azure Blob Storage
Document analysis is asynchronous and requires polling for operation completion
Prebuilt models support specific document types; custom forms require model training

useCases.targetUser: Finance teams and accounting software developers
useCases.title: Automated Invoice Processing
useCases.description: Extract vendor names, invoice dates, line items, and totals from supplier invoices. Integrate with accounts payable systems to automate data entry and reduce manual processing time.

useCases.targetUser: Mobile app developers building expense management tools
useCases.title: Receipt Data Capture
useCases.description: Build expense reporting applications that extract merchant names, transaction dates, and itemized purchases from receipt images. Enable mobile receipt scanning for employee reimbursements.

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise developers digitizing paper-based business processes
useCases.title: Custom Form Digitization
useCases.description: Create custom models for organization-specific forms like purchase orders, contracts, or survey responses. Train the model on sample documents and deploy automated extraction workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Start: Analyze a Document
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Java program using Azure Document Intelligence SDK to extract text and tables from a PDF file. Use the prebuilt-layout model and print the results to console.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner: First-time user wanting to test document analysis

promptTemplates.title: Receipt Analysis with Field Extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Java code to analyze a receipt image using the prebuilt-receipt model. Extract merchant name, transaction date, and total amount with confidence scores. Handle cases where fields may be missing.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Model for Invoice Processing
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate Java code to build a custom document model for invoice extraction. Include training from Azure Blob Storage, model composition from multiple invoice templates, and error handling for training failures.

promptTemplates.title: Document Classification Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete Java solution that classifies incoming documents as invoices, receipts, or contracts using a custom classifier, then routes each type to the appropriate prebuilt model for detailed extraction. Include async polling and retry logic.

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production deployments to leverage managed identities and avoid hardcoding credentials
Implement polling with appropriate timeouts for long-running analysis operations on large documents
Validate document format and size before submission to avoid unnecessary API calls and costs
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode API keys or endpoints in source code; use environment variables or Azure Key Vault
Avoid synchronous blocking calls in high-throughput scenarios; use async patterns for scalability
Do not process documents without validating file types; unsupported formats will fail analysis

faq.question: What document formats does Azure Form Recognizer support?
faq.answer: Azure Document Intelligence supports PDF, JPEG, PNG, BMP, and TIFF formats. PDF documents can be up to 500 pages. Maximum file size is 500 MB for PDF and 20 MB for images.

faq.question: How accurate are the prebuilt models?
faq.answer: Prebuilt models typically achieve 95%+ accuracy on standard documents. Accuracy varies by document quality, language, and field type. Custom models trained on your specific documents can achieve higher accuracy for specialized forms.

faq.question: What is the difference between prebuilt and custom models?
faq.answer: Prebuilt models work out-of-box for common document types like invoices and receipts. Custom models are trained on your specific document templates for fields not covered by prebuilt models, such as proprietary forms or specialized layouts.

faq.question: How do I handle documents in multiple languages?
faq.answer: Azure Document Intelligence supports over 70 languages automatically. Prebuilt models detect language from document content. For custom models, include training samples in all target languages for best results.

faq.question: What happens if the analysis operation fails?
faq.answer: Failed operations return an error with status code and message. Common errors include invalid document format, unsupported language, or service throttling. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures.

faq.question: Can I process documents stored in Azure Blob Storage directly?
faq.answer: Yes, you can pass a SAS URL to beginAnalyzeDocumentFromUrl instead of uploading file bytes. This is more efficient for large documents and reduces bandwidth usage. Ensure the SAS token has read permissions and adequate validity period.
